element_introduction_order.mastery_context: pilots introduced via cutscene establishing their role in rescue mission
alien spaceships introduced as primary threats
secondary defense mechanic - projectile that shoots enemies autonomously for duration
series-standard mechanic likely present but not explicitly documented in sources
established as primary antagonist force; professor kidnapping drives narrative

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: continuous horizontally-scrolling action with wave-based enemy encounters; forward momentum prevents backtracking

difficulty_curve.early_game: tutorial-like introduction to ship controls and basic enemies; establishes rescue mission narrative
notable_spikes: stage boss encounters (implied by shmup paradigm)
Famicom re-release added Easy mode option, suggesting original difficulty was considered challenging

notable_level_patterns.description: Player navigates through space fortress as primary setting; continuous horizontal scroll with enemies and obstacles; vertically-oriented screen despite horizontal movement
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All stages (fortress setting maintains across campaign)

notable_level_patterns.description: Enemies organized into attack waves; player must clear waves to progress through stage
Each stage advances toward rescuing Professor (Dr. Cinnamon); clear goal-driven structure from intro cutscene through ending

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor (Stinger NES/FDS documentation)
wikipedia - Stinger (1983 video game) - arcade predecessor for genre context
